body_excerpt: {"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-8347", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 2.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:P/PR:L/UI:N/VC:N/VI:L/VA:N/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-639", "name": "Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key"}], "description": "Concrete CMS 9.5.0 and below is vulnerable to IDOR + wrong-authorization-level in the Express association Reorder dialog.  This can cause Cross-entity state tampering with view-only permission on one entry. To be affected, a website has to be using express and relying on express entity ordering. Thanks Winston Crooker for reporting.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00175, "percentile": 0.07064}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-jqvq-gv67-3567", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-29T23:03:43Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-jqvq-gv67-3567", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-jqvq-gv67-3567"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-8347"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-22T15:16:26Z", "published_at": "2026-05-26T13:30:17Z", "references": ["https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-8347", "https://documentation.concretecms.org/9-x/developers/introduction/version-history/951-release-notes", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-jqvq-gv67-3567"], "repository_advisory_url": null, "severity": "low", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/concretecms/concretecms", "summary": "Concrete CMS is vulnerable to IDOR + wrong-authorization-level in the Express association Reorder dialog", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-29T23:03:44Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-jqvq-gv67-3567", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "9.5.1", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "concrete5/concrete5"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 9.5.1"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Concrete CMS is vulnerable to IDOR + wrong-authorization-level in the Express association Reorder dialog
